data.title: Old Dominion's 1Q Might Hold Positive Signs For The Economy — Market Talk
data.description: Old Dominion Freight Line's volumes this quarter appear to remain behind peers but are still tracking ahead of Benchmark's expectations, says analyst Christopher Kuhn. Management made positive commentary about the domestic economy earlier this month, "representing a shift from several quarters of emphasizing persistent macro softness, though geopolitical risks remain a consideration," Kuhn says. The company also continues to see gains in weight per shipment, which is "often an early indicator of improving underlying demand, particularly from industrial customers," he says. Due to recent trends in volume, yield and seasonal improvement, 1Q revenue could finish above the high end of management's guidance, Kuhn adds.(nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: The Government Isn't 'Insolvent.' Its Fiscal Picture Still Needs Fixing. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Casey Burgat

About the author: Casey Burgat is the director of the legislative affairs program at George Washington University's Graduate School of Political Management. He is author of We Hold These "Truths": How to Spot the Myths that are Holding America Back.

An alarming nugget buried in the Treasury Department's annual report on the federal government's books from the last fiscal year: Its long-term liabilities exceed its expected revenue by tens of trillions of dollars.

That prompted some in the media to declare that the U.S. is " insolvent." It is a striking claim. It is also a misleading one — at least as most people understand the term.

Insolvent usually describes a business or individual that can't pay its debts as they come due. But there is no imminent risk of the U.S. government running out of money in the way a company or household might. The U.S. continues to pay its obligations on time.

Framing the fiscal situation as "insolvency" may generate clicks, but it risks distracting from the real problem the report lays bare, which is that the fiscal imbalance is consistently getting worse.

The government's balance sheet shows total liabilities of about $47.8 trillion against roughly $6.1 trillion in assets, leaving a negative net position of about $41.7 trillion. That gap has widened over time, reflecting growing obligations that aren't matched by increasing revenue. Importantly, the liabilities don't even include social programs like Medicare and Social Security.

The numbers get more sobering as one reads on. Over the next 75 years, the government projects that its spending will exceed revenue by roughly $79.6 trillion. In a sign of how quickly the long-term outlook is deteriorating, that figure increased by nearly $7 trillion in just the past year. A large share of that future imbalance comes from the programs Americans rely on most. The projected shortfall in Social Security and Medicare alone is about $88 trillion over the same period.

Governments operate under different rules than private firms. They can adjust taxes, change benefits, restructure obligations, and crucially, borrow over long time horizons. The U.S. also benefits from the dollar's role as the world's reserve currency, which gives it far more fiscal flexibility than most countries.

So the government isn't insolvent today and it won't be tomorrow. But over time, its fiscal math will stop working.

The Treasury's projections show debt held by the public is already approaching the size of the entire U.S. economy — about 99% of gross domestic product today — and is rising dramatically under current policies. Interest costs are mounting as that debt accumulates. Meanwhile, demographic trends are pushing spending higher as more Americans retire and draw benefits for longer periods.

Some politicians deflect from taking action on this problem by saying faster economic growth will ease fiscal pressure over time by increasing revenue without raising tax rates. But growth alone won't close a gap of this magnitude. Fixing the problem will require the types of trade-offs elected officials have spent years avoiding.

On the spending side, they will likely need to revisit the structure of major entitlement programs. That doesn't necessarily require slashing benefits, but it could involve gradual changes — adjusting eligibility ages, modifying benefit formulas, or finding ways to control healthcare costs.

On the revenue side, it may mean raising taxes, broadening the tax base, or closing gaps in the system. The U.S. collects less in taxes as a share of its economy than many peer countries, even as it promises comparable or greater levels of spending.

Cutting benefits or raising taxes are difficult policy stances, especially in a polarized environment where compromise is hard to sustain, which explains why Washington has largely punted these issues into the future.

Instead of comprehensive reform, policymakers rely on temporary fixes, short-term funding patches, and optimistic assumptions about growth. The result is a slow and steady drift toward a more precarious fiscal positions that politicians can largely ignore in the short term.

But markets are reactive not just to current conditions but to expectations about the future. If confidence in the government's fiscal management over the long run erodes, borrowing costs could rise, putting additional pressure on the budget.

The Treasury's report should be understood as a warning light, not a doomsday clock. It is a reminder that the government's promises and its revenue are out of alignment, and that the longer that gap persists, the harder it will be to close. The sooner policymakers treat that as a governing problem instead of a political one, the more options they will have.

Wait too long, and the choices won't get easier. They'll just get made for us.

Guest commentaries like this one are written by authors outside the Barron's newsroom. They reflect the perspective and opinions of the authors. Submit feedback and commentary pitches to ideas@barrons.com .

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: The Board of Directors of Flerie has resolved to convert ordinary shares into series C shares under Flerie’s share redemption scheme
data.description:
At the Extraordinary General Meeting of Flerie AB (publ) (“Flerie” or the “Company”) on 10 June 2024, it was resolved to implement a share redemption scheme. Redemption under the scheme is regulated in the Articles of Association and is carried out in two steps, conversion of ordinary shares to shares of series C followed by redemption of the shares of series C.

During the conversion period from 24 March 2026 up to and including 31 March 2026, 9,571,328 ordinary shares were submitted for conversion, representing approximately eleven (11) percent of Flerie’s total share capital. According to the Articles of Association, the maximum number of ordinary shares eligible for conversion is capped at a level requiring that the total outstanding shares of series C, following the completion of the conversion, constitute no more than five (5) percent of the Company’s total share capital. If shareholders submit ordinary shares for conversion in excess of this threshold, the number of shares to be converted shall be allocated proportionally to each shareholder’s total request for conversion.

Flerie hereby announces that the Board of Directors, in accordance with the conversion provision in the Articles of Association, has resolved to convert 4,380,078 ordinary shares into an equivalent number of shares of series C, corresponding to five (5) percent of the Company’s total share capital. Shareholders who submitted ordinary shares during the conversion period have consequently had approximately 45 percent of their requested shares converted, based on the number of ordinary shares each shareholder submitted for conversion. To the extent that the distribution did not go out evenly, distribution took place by drawing of lots.

The conversion is deemed completed once it has been registered with the Swedish Companies Registration Office and recorded in the share register.

After the conversion has been completed, the total number of shares and votes in Flerie will remain 87,601,573. The total number of shares will consist of 83,221,495 ordinary shares and 4,380,078 shares of series C.

Shares of series C carry one vote per share and do not entitle their holders to receive dividends.

As soon as practicable after the publication of the Company’s interim report for the first quarter of 2026, the Board of Directors intends to resolve on the redemption of all outstanding shares of series C. The redemption amount for each share of series C will correspond to its net asset value as of 31 March 2026, as reported in the interim report for the first quarter of 2026.

For more information, please contact:

Ted Fjällman, CEO of Flerie

E-mail:ir@flerie.com

Telephone: +46 (0)76 600 89 26

The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact person above, on 31 March 2026, at 19:05 CEST.

About Flerie

Flerie is an active long-term life science investor, with a broad and diversified portfolio of innovative companies based on pioneering science. We invest in product development and commercial growth opportunities globally alongside other leading investors, focusing predominantly on private companies that are otherwise difficult to access. Flerie’s active ownership model, broad network and resources support and accelerate the development of the portfolio projects, creating value for shareholders. Flerie AB’s ordinary share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm with the ticker FLERIE. For further information please visitwww.flerie.com.

Attachments

The Board of Directors of Flerie has resolved to convert ordinary shares into series C shares under Flerie’s share redemption scheme

data.title: Analysts See Increase in U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

Analysts expect a sixth consecutive weekly build in U.S. crude oil inventories, while gasoline stocks are seen extending their decline, according to a survey by The Wall Street Journal.

Commercial crude stocks are expected to have risen by 800,000 barrels to 457 million barrels in the week ended March 27, according to the average estimate of nine analysts and traders. Five predict an increase and four expect a drop. Estimates range from a build of 4.1 million barrels to a draw of 2.2 million barrels.

Gasoline inventories are forecast to have fallen by 1.5 million barrels to 239.9 million barrels, with estimates ranging from a 2 million barrel increase to a decline of 4.4 million barrels.

Stocks of distillate fuel, mostly diesel, are seen down by 300,000 barrels at 119.6 million barrels, with forecasts ranging from a drop of 3 million barrels to a rise of 2.9 million barrels.

Refinery capacity use likely rose by half of a percentage point to 93.4%. Forecasts range from a 1 percentage point rise to a 0.4 percentage point decline. Two analysts didn't forecast refinery runs.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release the inventory data Wednesday at 10:30 a.m. ET.

 
                                    Crude    Gasoline  Distillates Refinery Use 
   Again Capital                     2.8       -1.9       -2.6        0.4 
   Confluence Investment Management  2.0       -1.5       -1.5        1.0 
   Rystad Energy                    -1.3       -4.4       -1.1       -0.4 
   Excel Futures                     4.1       -2.8        2.9        0.7 
   Spartan Capital Securities        3.4        0.9        1.7        n/f 
   Mizuho                           -2.0       -1.0        1.0        0.5 
   Price Futures Group               2.0        2.0        2.0        1.0 
   Ritterbusch and Associates       -1.3       -2.5       -3.0        0.5 
   Tradition Energy                 -2.2       -2.1       -2.4        n/f 
 
   AVERAGE                           0.8       -1.5       -0.3        0.5

Note: Numbers in millions of barrels, with the exception of refinery use, which is in percentage points.

n/f = no forecast

unch = unchanged

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: JD Power: New Vehicle Sales Outlook Steady Despite War in Iran — Market Talk
data.description: JD Power is holding its outlook for new vehicle sales in 2026 steady at 16.3 million, even as the war in Iran drives up the price of fuel. "Demand for new vehicles remains robust, despite economic uncertainty associated with the current situation in the Middle East and rising fuel prices," JD Power OEM Solutions President Thomas King says. He says that the effect of higher fuel prices is softened by the better fuel economy of modern vehicles, adding that car buyers have become less vulnerable to price volatility. "There is no doubt that the current situation in the Middle East will make things more difficult to predict over the near-term, and the longer-term risks to auto sales are material, but the fundamentals are strong for a solid year in 2026," King says. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Paris city hall raided in connection with contract awarded under former mayor Hidalgo
data.description:
Searches were carried out on Tuesday at various locations, including Paris City Hall on suspicion of bias in the award of a contract under former mayor Anne Hidalgo, the country's national financial prosecutor's (PNF) office said in a statement.

  • The searches are part of a preliminary investigation opened by the PNF on February 6 on charges including favouritism and aiding and abetting favouritism, the statement said.

  • The investigation was launched after a report by the Paris Police to the PNF concerning the appointment of the artistic director for the ceremony commemorating the 10th anniversary of the Paris attacks of November 13, 2015.

  • The Paris City Hall and former Paris mayor Hidalgo could not be immediately reached for comment.

  • Socialist Party candidate Emmanuel Gregoire beat conservative former minister Rachida Dati in the Paris mayoral race on Sunday March 22, succeeding Hidalgo.

data.title: Scott Andrews Has Been Named Head of Specialized Industries For BOK Financial
data.description:
TULSA, OK / ACCESS Newswire / March 31, 2026 / Scott Andrews has been promoted to fill the role of specialized industries banking executive for BOK Financial, effective Dec. 1, following the retirement of Brad Vincent.

In this role, Andrews will lead BOK Financial's specialized business, including commercial real estate, commercial strategies, dealer financial services, energy, healthcare, loan syndications and TransFund.

"Scott brings deep institutional knowledge and a genuine commitment to both our employees and clients," said Stacy Kymes, president and CEO of BOK Financial. "He understands what makes these businesses successful and leads with consistency and integrity that earns trust."

Andrews said, "BOK Financial's specialized industries are built upon decades of experience. What differentiates us from our competitors is our long-term commitment to the industries we serve, which has allowed us to recruit and develop extraordinary banking teams with deep expertise. We are market leaders in these segments because our clients have witnessed our commitment to their business, especially in challenging market conditions, and trust our bankers' counsel. I'm grateful for the opportunity to work alongside this remarkable team."

Andrews has served as executive director for BOK Financial's healthcare banking division since 2022. Prior to that he served as the chief operating officer for the Commercial Division since 2017. In that role, he was directly responsible for division-wide strategic initiatives-including loan syndications, client service, customer-facing digital experiences, and front-line technology investments. Scott has previously worked in a variety of credit and corporate development roles across the organization. He joined BOK Financial through its Accelerated Career Track development program.

Andrews holds a bachelor's degree in accounting from the University of Notre Dame. Active in the community, he currently serves on the Ascension St. John Foundation board and has previously supported Tulsa Regional Tourism, 108 Contemporary and Catholic Charities of Eastern Oklahoma.

About BOK Financial

BOK Financial Corporation is a $52 billion regional financial services company headquartered in Tulsa, Oklahoma with $127 billion in assets under management and administration. The company's stock is publicly traded on NASDAQ under the Global Select market listings (BOKF). BOK Financial Corporation's holdings include BOKF, NA; BOK Financial Securities, Inc.; and BOK Financial Private Wealth, Inc. BOKF, NA's holdings include TransFund and Cavanal Hill Investment Management, Inc. BOKF, NA operates banking divisions across eight states as: Bank of Albuquerque; Bank of Oklahoma; Bank of Texas; and BOK Financial in Arizona, Arkansas, Colorado, Kansas and Missouri; as well as having limited purpose offices in Connecticut, Nebraska, Tennessee and Wisconsin. Through its subsidiaries, BOK Financial Corporation provides commercial and consumer banking, brokerage trading, investment, trust and insurance services, mortgage origination and servicing, and an electronic funds transfer network.

Contact:

Heather King

Director of Investor Relations

214.676.4666

SOURCE: BOK Financial

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: JM selling residential project in Sollentuna, Stockholm
data.description:
JM has signed an agreement with Folksam for the sale of a rental project in Väsjön, Sollentuna. The sales price amounts to SEK 580m.

The property being sold is the Väsjö Port project (Ekosystemet 1 and 4), consisting of 165 residential units with a total of 9,352 square meters of residential area. The property is part of the larger Väsjön development area in the eastern part of Sollentuna Municipality, located approximately 15 kilometers north of Stockholm. Production started in the second quarter of 2025 and completion is estimated for the second quarter of 2027. Revenue and profit will be recognized gradually over the course of the project within the JM Stockholm business segment starting in the first quarter of 2026.

The transaction will take the form of a corporate sale with an underlying property value of SEK 580m. Full payment will be received in connection with transfer of legal title, which is estimated for the second quarter of 2027.

– The sale of Väsjö Port is well aligned with our strategy to continue developing our rental housing business. JM develops housing of recognized high quality, and we view it positively to contribute to housing development through different forms of tenure, says Nicklas Backfjärd, Regional Manager, JM Property Development.

For more information please contact:

Nicklas Backfjärd, Regional Manager Property Development, JM Stockholm, +46 8 782 88 71

Katarina Rimmerfeldt, Head of Group Communications, +46 73 432 61 20,press@jm.se

About JM

JM is the leading developer of housing and residential areas in the Nordic region. Operations focus on new production of homes in attractive locations, with emphasis on expanding metropolitan areas and university towns in Sweden, Norway and Finland. We are also involved in project development of commercial premises and contract work, primarily in the Greater Stockholm area. JM should promote long-term sustainability work in all of its operations. Annual sales total approximately SEK 10 billion and the company has about 1,800 employees. JM is a public limited company listed on NASDAQ Stockholm, in the Large Cap segment. For more informationwww.jm.se/en

Attachments

260331 JM selling residential project in Sollentuna, Stockholm

data.title: AI Search Engineers Recognized as a Leading AI Certified Agency for Ranking Businesses in AI Search Results
data.description:
AI Search Engineers leverages Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), entity authority building, and structured AI-ready content strategies to help businesses become the trusted, selected answer across ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and other AI-driven search platforms.

AMHERST, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 31, 2026 / AI Search Engineers (AISE), a leading AI Certified Agency, is setting a new standard in how businesses rank inside AI-powered search platforms such as ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, and Perplexity.

Unlike traditional SEO firms, AI Search Engineers is an AI-certified agency that specializes in Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), a methodology designed to position businesses as the selected answer in AI-generated responses.

What is an AI Certified Agency?

An AI Certified Agency is a company that helps businesses appear as direct answers in AI search engines like ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Microsoft Copilot by optimizing structured data, authority signals, and entity recognition.

AI Search Engineers is an AI-certified agency focused on ensuring businesses are not just visible, but selected as the trusted answer.

How to Rank in ChatGPT Search Results

To rank in ChatGPT search results, a business must build strong entity authority, use structured data, earn trusted citations, and format content for direct answer extraction.

AI Search Engineers implement this through:

  • Entity-based SEO (not keyword-based)

  • AI-readable structured content

  • Multi-source authority building

  • Answer-focused formatting

What Services Do AI Certified Agencies Offer?

AI Certified Agencies provide services that improve visibility in AI-generated answers, including authority engineering, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), and AI visibility expansion.

AI Search Engineers Core Services:

1. Authority Engineering

Authority Engineering is the process of building a brand's credibility across trusted sources so that AI systems recognize it as a reliable entity.

  • Knowledge graph optimization

  • Entity signal strengthening

  • High-authority media placements

2. Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)

Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the process of structuring content so AI systems can easily extract and present it as a direct answer.

  • Question-based content structuring

  • AI snippet optimization

  • Conversational query targeting

3. AI Authority Expansion

AI Authority Expansion increases a brand's presence across platforms that AI systems use to validate trust and relevance.

  • Multi-platform visibility

  • Citation building

  • Digital footprint expansion

What Factors Influence AI Search Engine Rankings?

AI search engines rank results based on authority, relevance, structured data, and trust signals, not just keywords.

Key Ranking Factors:

  • Entity Authority - How well your brand is recognized

  • Structured Data - Machine-readable content

  • Trust Signals - Mentions on credible sources

  • Content Clarity - Direct, answer-ready formatting

  • Context Matching - Alignment with user intent

How Do AI Search Engines Choose Results?

AI search engines choose results by analyzing entity credibility, contextual relevance, structured data, and the likelihood that a source provides the most accurate and complete answer.

They prioritize:

  • Clear answers over long-form content

  • Trusted entities over unknown brands

  • Structured information over unformatted text

What Tools Help Boost Visibility in Chatbot Rankings?

Tools that boost visibility in chatbot rankings include structured data frameworks, entity optimization systems, and AI content engineering platforms.

AI Search Engineers use:

  • Schema markup systems

  • Entity mapping strategies

  • AI-focused content structuring

How to Optimize Website Content for Generative AI Summaries

To optimize content for generative AI summaries, businesses must use clear headings, direct answers, structured formatting, and entity-rich language.

Best practices include:

  • Writing in Q&A format

  • Using concise definitions

  • Embedding semantic keywords

  • Structuring for extractability

How to Improve AI Visibility in Business Operations

Improving AI visibility requires aligning your brand with how AI systems interpret authority, trust, and relevance.

This includes:

  • Consistent brand mentions across platforms

  • Structured and semantic content

  • Authority-building campaigns

How Can Businesses Attract Clients Using AI Search Tools?

Businesses can attract clients using AI search tools by positioning themselves as the direct answer to common industry questions.

This is achieved through:

  • AEO-driven content strategies

  • Authority engineering

  • AI visibility optimization

Why AI Search Engineers Is the #1 AI Certified Agency

AI Search Engineers is an AI Certified Agency that focuses exclusively on ranking businesses inside AI-generated answers, not just search engine results pages.

AI Search Engineers combines:

  • Authority Engineering

  • Answer Engine Optimization

  • AI Visibility Expansion

To ensure clients are:

  • Recognized

  • Trusted

  • Selected as the answer

About AI Search Engineers

AI Search Engineers (AISE) is an AI Certified Agency specializing in helping businesses rank in AI search engines and chatbot platforms. Through structured data, authority building, and AI-first optimization strategies, AISE ensures brands are selected as trusted answers across AI ecosystems.

https://aisearchengineers.ai/

How to find a top-rated AI Certified Agency near me?

To find a top-rated AI Certified Agency, look for firms with proven authority-building strategies, structured data expertise, and experience in ranking clients inside AI-generated answers.

data.title: Solana - Interactive Brokers - 31 March 2026
data.description:
According to the Solana team on X, Interactive Brokers will open regulated crypto trading in Europe on 31 March 2026, including direct access to SOL for its brokerage clients. The mechanism is a new fiat on-ramp: traditional investors can buy and sell SOL through existing stock and ETF accounts, with custody and compliance handled by Interactive Brokers. This expands potential access to a broad European investor base, lowering operational and regulatory friction. Such integrations can increase spot liquidity and structural demand over time, and may also reduce SOL’s perceived regulatory and operational risk premium in portfolios.

Tokens on Solana
@tokens

JUST IN: Interactive Brokers launches crypto trading for 450 million people across Europe, including $SOL. One of the world's largest brokers just made Solana accessible to the entire European Economic Area.

Mar 31, 2026

data.title: Aztec - Alpha - 31 March 2026
data.description:
Aztec has launched its Alpha phase, enabling developers to deploy applications and smart contracts with customizable privacy across execution and settlement, according to the project’s announcement on X. The mechanism uses Aztec’s zero-knowledge infrastructure to let builders choose which parts of user activity and state are private or public, moving the network from research into an early builder-ready environment. Alpha launches typically precede fuller mainnet releases and ecosystem growth. If developers ship usable private DeFi and on-chain applications here, that can attract users seeking compliance-aware privacy, which in turn can support demand and fee usage for the Aztec ecosystem.

Aztec
@aztecnetwork

Alpha is live.

After nearly a decade, the first feature-complete privacy stack on Ethereum is here.

Developers can now build apps and contracts with ground-up customizable privacy, from execution to settlement.https://t.co/ITasZpe0yM pic.twitter.com/68nqFujcj9

data.title: Across Protocol - U.S. C Corporation Vote - From 31 March to 07 April 2026
data.description:
Across Protocol governance is voting from 31 March to 7 April 2026 on a proposal to transition from a token-based DAO into a U.S. C corporation. As described in the proposal summary on X (AcrossProtocol), protocol control, intellectual property, and revenue rights would move toward a formal corporate entity, with token governance powers potentially reduced or redefined. The mechanism matters because markets often reprice tokens when decision-making and value capture shift away from token holders. A successful vote could expand legal and fundraising options, but may lead traders to question the long-term role and upside of ACX.

Across
@AcrossProtocol

The Bridge Across: ACX Token-to-Equity Exchange & Buyout Proposal is now live.

This proposal seeks approval to transition Across Protocol from a token-based DAO structure to a U.S. C corporation.$ACX token holders have 7 days to vote.

Vote here: https://t.co/YTIjExVp3i pic.twitter.com/WAyV9tqKlr

MCLEAN, Va.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 31, 2026--

Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. will report first quarter 2026 financial results before the stock market opens on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, followed by a conference call at 9 a.m. EDT. Christopher J. Nassetta, president & chief executive officer, Hilton, and Kevin Jacobs, executive vice president & chief financial officer, Hilton, will discuss the company's performance and lead a question-and-answer session.

Participants may listen to the live webcast by logging on to the Hilton Investor Relations website at https://ir.hilton.com/events-and-presentations. A replay and transcript of the webcast will be available within 24 hours of the live event at https://ir.hilton.com/financial-reporting. Alternatively, participants may listen to the live call by dialing 1-888-317-6003 in the United States or 1-412-317-6061 internationally using the conference ID 9497198. Participants are encouraged to dial into the call or link to the webcast at least fifteen minutes prior to the scheduled start time. A telephone replay will be available for seven days following the call. To access the telephone replay, dial 1-855-669-9658 in the United States or 1-412-317-0088 internationally using the conference ID 3095590.

About Hilton

Hilton is a leading global hospitality company with a portfolio of 27 world-class brands comprising more than 9,100 properties and over 1.3 million rooms, in 143 countries and territories. Dedicated to fulfilling its founding vision to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality, Hilton has welcomed over 4 billion guests in its more than 100-year history. Named as the No. 1 World's Best Workplace by Great Place to Work and Fortune, Hilton aims to create the best culture for its 500,000 team members around the world. Hilton has introduced industry-leading technology enhancements to improve the guest experience, including Digital Key Share, automated complimentary room upgrades and the ability to book confirmed connecting rooms. Through the award-winning guest loyalty program Hilton Honors, the nearly 250 million Hilton Honors members who book directly with Hilton can earn Points for hotel stays and experiences money can't buy. With the free Hilton Honors app, guests can book their stay, select their room, check in, unlock their door with a Digital Key and check out, all from their smartphone. Visit stories.hilton.com for more information, and connect with Hilton on Facebook, X, LinkedIn, Instagram and YouTube.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260331983455/en/

data.title: Equity Capital Markets Activity Expected to Cool on Middle East Conflict
data.description:
By Joe Stonor

Funds raised by companies through equity capital markets leapt on year in the first quarter of 2026, but war in the Middle East is expected to have a chilling impact on companies' ability to raise money.

Global companies raised $256.8 billion via equity-related fundraising in the first quarter, according to a report from data analytics group Mergermarket.

U.S. companies led the way, raising $121.8 billion, a 60% on-year increase. Around $45 billion was raised via IPOs alone, a 40% hike on the same period last year.

However, the Middle East conflict will have a chilling impact on equity fundraising in the near future, the report said.

"You are dealing with quite a significant period of geopolitical and macroeconomic volatility now. It's never good to launch anything into that sort of market," said Sam Kerr, Mergermarket's head of global equity capital markets.

Companies mulling a public offering typically seek to take advantage of rising stock markets. The broad-based selloff that followed the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran sent a host of U.S. and European equities into correction territory, making listing less attractive.

"When you're dealing with an IPO, you don't really want to be pushing a deal at a time where investors are experiencing a lot of pain in their portfolios," Kerr said.

Economic uncertainty also means that companies will also have a harder time confidently putting together earnings forecasts--the key metric by which analysts determine IPO valuations.

European companies lagged far behind their U.S. peers in fundraising volumes in the first quarter, according to the report. However, European private companies will increasingly look to their home markets for public listings given the better track records of companies that opted not to pursue a U.S. listing, Kerr said.

"Over the longer term, European businesses that list in Europe have outperformed European businesses that listed in the U.S. The attractiveness of Europe and home market listings versus going to the States is growing at a pretty impressive rate," he said.

Potential listing to watch out for in Europe include Elliott Investment Management-owned booksellers Waterstones and Barnes & Noble, and software group Visma, Kerr said. Elliott Asset Management and Visma did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Write to Joe Stonor at josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com

data.title: American Heart Association urges people to favor plant-based proteins, replace full fat dairy
data.description:
The American Heart Association's new nutrition guidance, released on Tuesday, emphasized a dietary pattern rich in vegetables, fruits and whole grains, prioritizing plant-based protein over meat, while limiting sugar, salt and ultra-processed foods and replacing full fat dairy with non-fat and low-fat dairy.

U.S. President Donald Trump earlier this year announced a new slate of dietary guidelines that recommended Americans eat more protein from both animal and plant sources, and encouraged the consumption of full-fat dairy.

In contrast to the federal government's recommendation of use of beef tallow and consumption of red meat, the AHA pushed for plant-based proteins such as legumes, nuts and seeds, choosing low-fat or fat-free dairy, and, when consuming red meat, selecting lean cuts, avoiding processed forms and keeping portions small.

The Trump administration and Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s push to "Make America Healthy Again" has focused on the removal of synthetic dyes, other chemical ingredients, ultraprocessed foods, consumption of less sugar and seed oils.

The AHA also suggested Americans choose sources of unsaturated fats, avoid ultraprocessed foods, and minimize intake of added sugars in beverages and foods.

The organization recommended Americans to prepare foods with minimal or no salt, and limit the intake of alcohol.

The guidance also recommended children can and should begin following a heart-healthy dietary pattern starting at 1 year of age.

The AHA, which is the nation's oldest and largest voluntary organization dedicated to fighting heart disease and stroke, releases dietary guidance about every five years to promote cardiovascular health.
